contact_lookup_status: No named recruiter was visible on the official Bosch posting checked on 2026-03-29. The posting lists Olaf Autzen as the functional contact, not the recruiter, so the letter uses Hiring Team as the application contact.
job_title: Working Student Internal Web Developed Applications in Automotive Quality Environment (f/m/div.)
subject_line: Application for Working Student Internal Web Applications
why_company: Bosch stands out to me because this role combines internal web applications, SQL-backed process support, low-code development, and quality-management reporting in a structured automotive environment where technical execution and operational reliability matter.
job_description: Bosch is hiring a working student for internal web developed applications in automotive quality environment in Reutlingen-Kusterdingen. The role supports risk analysis and quality reporting, maintenance and expansion of associated SQL databases, independent troubleshooting in existing applications with user-focused validation, and enhancement of existing low-code applications based on Outsystems. The role is part-time for 12 months at 10 hours per week starting in April 2026.
requirements: Studies in computer science, data science, or a comparable field
Experience with HTML/JavaScript, Python, Java, relational databases (SQL), and low-code development
Creativity and enthusiasm for web applications and their development
Business-fluent German and English, written and spoken
cv_about_override: Business analysis and digital operations professional building stronger technical depth in internal web applications, SQL-backed workflows, structured problem-solving, and data-informed process improvement.
cv_summary_override: Experienced business analysis and product professional with a strong foundation in research, requirements, stakeholder alignment, rollout, and continuous improvement. Alongside my earlier business background, I am building deeper technical capability through Software Engineering studies at Heilbronn University and hands-on work across SQL, automation, digital operations, and AI-assisted tools. This makes me well suited for working student roles that combine internal web applications, data-backed process support, troubleshooting, and continuous improvement in structured quality environments.
cover_letter_opening: I am applying for the position of Working Student Internal Web Developed Applications in Automotive Quality Environment (f/m/div.) at Robert Bosch GmbH. The role stands out to me because it combines internal web applications, SQL-backed processes, troubleshooting, and low-code development in a structured quality environment.
cover_letter_body_one: In my previous roles across digital products and operations-heavy environments, I worked with stakeholders, engineers, and business teams to structure requirements, improve workflows, and support practical digital solutions. This gave me a strong foundation in structured problem-solving, user-focused improvement, and reliable execution.
cover_letter_body_two: Through my Software Engineering studies at Heilbronn University, my Business Information Systems background, and hands-on work across SQL, automation, digital operations, and AI-assisted tools, I have been intentionally moving toward more technical and systems-oriented work. This is why I am particularly interested in contributing to internal applications, database-backed processes, and iterative improvements in close collaboration with users and quality teams.
cover_letter_motivation: Bosch is especially attractive to me because of its high engineering standards and practical internal digitalization work. Supporting quality reporting, risk-analysis processes, SQL databases, and low-code applications is closely aligned with the technical direction I want to keep building.
cover_letter_closing: @@AVAILABILITY@@. My German is still improving and not yet business fluent, but I am actively learning and work confidently in English at C1 level with IELTS 7.5. If this role is not the best fit, I would be grateful to be considered for another suitable working student opportunity at Bosch. I would welcome the opportunity to discuss my application in an interview.
